rules.name: Database Schema
rules.description: Documentation of the database schema for the Marketing Calendar SaaS application
rules.content:
# Marketing Calendar SaaS Database Schema

## Tables

### Users
- id (UUID, PK): Unique identifier
- name (TEXT): User's full name
- email (TEXT, unique): User's email address
- role (TEXT): 'admin', 'manager', or 'contributor'
- created_at (TIMESTAMP): Creation timestamp
- avatar_url (TEXT, optional): URL to avatar image

### Resources
- id (UUID, PK): Unique identifier
- name (TEXT): Resource name
- type (TEXT): 'internal', 'agency', or 'freelancer'
- created_at (TIMESTAMP): Creation timestamp

### Campaigns
- id (UUID, PK): Unique identifier
- name (TEXT): Campaign name
- campaign_type (TEXT): 'tradeshow', 'product_launch', 'seasonal_promotion', 'digital_campaign', 'event', or 'other'
- start_date (DATE): Campaign start date
- end_date (DATE): Campaign end date
- location (TEXT, optional): Physical location
- description (TEXT, optional): Campaign description
- created_at (TIMESTAMP): Creation timestamp
- updated_at (TIMESTAMP): Last update timestamp

### Briefs
- id (UUID, PK): Unique identifier
- title (TEXT): Brief title
- channel (TEXT): Marketing channel
- start_date (DATE): Work start date
- due_date (DATE): Due date
- resource_id (UUID, FK, optional): Reference to resources.id
- approver_id (UUID, FK, optional): Reference to users.id
- campaign_id (UUID, FK): Reference to campaigns.id
- status (TEXT): 'draft', 'pending_approval', 'approved', 'in_progress', 'review', 'complete', or 'cancelled'
- priority (TEXT): 'low', 'medium', 'high', or 'urgent'
- description (TEXT, optional): Brief description
- specifications (JSONB, optional): Detailed specifications
- estimated_hours (NUMERIC, optional): Estimated hours
- expenses (NUMERIC, optional): Estimated/actual expenses
- created_by (UUID, FK): Reference to users.id
- created_at (TIMESTAMP): Creation timestamp
- updated_at (TIMESTAMP): Last update timestamp

## Relationships
- User → Briefs: One-to-many (creator)
- User → Briefs: One-to-many (approver)
- Resource → Briefs: One-to-many
- Campaign → Briefs: One-to-many

For full documentation, see SCHEMA_DOCUMENTATION.md

rules.name: Database Queries
rules.description: Common database queries for the Marketing Calendar SaaS application
rules.content:
# Common Database Queries

## Campaigns

### Get all campaigns
```typescript
const { data, error } = await supabase.from('campaigns').select('*');
```

### Get campaign by ID
```typescript
const { data, error } = await supabase.from('campaigns').select('*').eq('id', campaignId).single();
```

### Get campaigns by type
```typescript
const { data, error } = await supabase.from('campaigns').select('*').eq('campaign_type', 'tradeshow');
```

### Get upcoming campaigns
```typescript
const { data, error } = await supabase.from('campaigns').select('*').gte('start_date', new Date().toISOString());
```

## Briefs

### Get all briefs
```typescript
const { data, error } = await supabase.from('briefs').select('*');
```

### Get brief by ID
```typescript
const { data, error } = await supabase.from('briefs').select('*').eq('id', briefId).single();
```

### Get briefs by campaign
```typescript
const { data, error } = await supabase.from('briefs').select('*').eq('campaign_id', campaignId);
```

### Get briefs by status
```typescript
const { data, error } = await supabase.from('briefs').select('*').eq('status', 'draft');
```

### Get briefs with related data
```typescript
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('briefs')
  .select(`
    *,
    campaigns(*),
    resources(*),
    users!briefs_created_by_fkey(*),
    approvers:users!briefs_approver_id_fkey(*)
  `);
```
